what_it_does: This file is an interpretive ledger entry for the Architecture.jsx canvas. It records a neutral, file-local reading of what the component does, how it consumes window.CV_REGISTRY, which parts are live projections versus hardcoded prose, and its apparent themes, contribution, and embedding summary.
observations: The entry describes a React canvas that renders a live, registry-driven explanation of the Universal Type Registry.
It notes that layer counts, slot schemas, lineage trees, and cross-embedding examples are read from window.CV_REGISTRY, while ConsumersMatrix and EntryPoints are hardcoded descriptive arrays.
It lists specific type ids the canvas depends on, such as system.widget.hybrid, doc.wizard, doc.deck, doc.widget, surface.deck-slide.content, and block.callout.
It records that the canvas renders real TypeThumb previews when available, guarded by window.TypeThumb checks.
It flags a few inline style objects and soft null-fallback behavior when expected types are missing.
The file exports no code symbols; symbols[] is explicitly empty.
standouts: The interpretive entry itself is a structured, machine-readable summary of a UI component that is also a live projection of the registry it documents.
It clearly separates live-derived registry content from hand-written descriptive content within the canvas.
conventions: Follows the interpretive ledger schema with project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, and intention_for_embedding.
Remains file-local and neutral, avoiding cross-file comparisons or rankings.
Uses plain factual language and tight arrays.
concerns: The entry notes that hardcoded ConsumersMatrix and EntryPoints arrays can drift from actual surfaces and creation flows because they are not derived from the registry.
It observes that missing specific type ids cause silent null renders rather than visible failure signals.
notes: A neutral interpretive summary of the Architecture.jsx canvas, capturing its live registry projection, hardcoded explanatory sections, and the self-documenting-system theme.
questions: What is the exact shape of LAYER_INFO and the fixed LAYERS ordering used by the ladder?
Are ConsumersMatrix and EntryPoints intended to stay as curated prose or to become registry-derived in the future?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the entry states it is an interpretive read of a canvas that explains the Universal Type Registry, and its fields consistently describe that purpose.
apparent_themes: Self-documenting system (the canvas explains the registry by projecting it live)
intention_signals: To capture a stable, queryable interpretation of the Architecture canvas so downstream fusion can understand what it documents, what it derives, and where it relies on hardcoded assumptions.
novelty: An interpretive layer that documents a documentation canvas whose content is partially live-projected from the same registry it explains.
contribution: Provides a structured, neutral reading of Architecture.jsx that preserves the distinction between registry-derived and hardcoded content, lists dependent type ids, and supplies embedding-ready summaries for downstream fusion.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger entry for the Architecture.jsx React canvas, describing its live projection of the Universal Type Registry, hardcoded ConsumersMatrix and EntryPoints, dependent type ids, and self-documenting-system theme.
intention_for_embedding: To record a neutral, file-local interpretation of the Architecture canvas so fusion queries can understand its live-derived versus hand-written parts and its role as a visual explainer of the registry.
